channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Automating Bulk Content Authoring in SitecoreAI with PowerShell Extensions
item.description: Modern CMS platforms excel at managing content, but large-scale content updates remain one of the biggest operational challenges for content teams. While SitecoreAI provides powerful...

item.description: Imagine You're Standing Outside a Shop... Have you ever noticed something interesting when you walk past a shop? Even before entering, you can see the shop's name board. "Fresh Mart" You don't...

item.title: Selling a macOS app outside the App Store is easy. Licensing is the hard part.
item.description: A lot of indie macOS developers eventually ask the same question: “Should I sell through the Mac App Store, or sell directly?” Selling directly sounds simple at first. You add Stripe, Paddle,...

item.title: The Agent That Actually Remembers You: A Deep Dive into Hermes Agent published
item.description: This is a submission for the Hermes Agent Challenge I'll be honest — I was skeptical. Every few weeks something in the AI space gets described as "turning heads quietly" and it turns out to be a...

item.title: What Is HTTP & HTTPS ?
item.description: Every time you open a browser and visit a website, something invisible but essential happens in the background — your browser and the server are having a conversation. The rules that govern that...

item.title: 007 First Light is already discounted for the PS5 and Steam
item.description: IO Interactive’s 007 First Light is here, and it’s just as stunning a James Bond mov — err, video game — as we hoped it would be. Pardon the confusion, the title’s engaging tutorial really feels...

item.title: Marathon’s second season is a chance for Bungie to turn things around
item.description: Earlier this month, I finally achieved the elusive goal I had set for myself in Bungie's Marathon. I collected six of the game's rarest items, allowing me to attempt and then successfully clear...

item.title: Frontend Architecture: Where Does This File Go?
item.description: The PR That Started A Twenty-Comment War The code was fine. Logic was correct. TypeScript was happy. Tests passed. And then the review comment landed: "Where does this actually belong?" That was...

item.description: The contemporary industrial landscape demands robust management frameworks in the face of extreme volatility. At the epicenter of this organizational and analytical architecture stands the...

item.title: Vue Teleport component React: How does VuReact convert it?
item.description: VuReact is a compiler toolchain for migrating from Vue to React — and for writing React with Vue 3 syntax. In this article, we dive straight into the core: how Vue's built-in <Teleport> component...

item.title: How to audit an AI agent skill: the 7-check framework we used on 200 skills
item.description: {/* JSON-LD generated server-side in app/blog/[slug]/page.tsx; inline {...} blocks crash MDX's Acorn parser on the leading {. */} TL;DR This is the full methodology we use to audit AI agent skills...

item.title: AI for Knowledge Management: Real Workflows That Hold Up
item.description: AI is not replacing knowledge management; it is changing the shape of it for both individuals and teams. Microsoft's Work Trend Index describes a move toward hybrid teams of humans and agents, and...

item.title: A meteor exploded off the coast of Boston
item.description: On Saturday, at around 2:06 pm ET, a meteor streaked over the northeastern US and exploded north of Cape Cod Bay. The fireball was caught on camera by several people, shook houses, and can even be...

item.title: I went looking for the AI weed vape that gives you Bitcoin for smoking
item.description: The crypto weed vape found me on 4/20, the high holiday of cannabis enthusiasts everywhere. It arrived over Slack with the thumbnail of a man exhaling a plume of vapor, the words "every hit...

item.title: Dutch Authorities Dismantle Botnet Linked to 17 Million Infected Devices
item.description: Dutch authorities have announced the takedown of a botnet that enslaved millions of infected devices, including computers, tablets, smartphones, and IoT devices, to carry out malicious attacks....

item.title: User-replaceable batteries are coming back in a big way
item.description: This is The Stepback, a weekly newsletter breaking down one essential story from the tech world. For more news about gadgets and smartphones, follow Dominic Preston. The Stepback arrives in our...

item.title: The Mercedes CLA offers great EV specs for an average price
item.description: Despite headwinds from the current administration, automakers continue to release well-equipped EVs with bigger battery packs and increasingly faster charging speeds. For those who want to travel...

item.title: How Turkey Hacked the Hair Transplant Industry
item.description: From specialized motors to the use of machine learning algorithms, Turkey’s billion-dollar hair-transplant industry is the result of a constant process of innovation.

item.title: SoftBank says it will invest up to €75 billion to build French data centers
item.description: The goal, the firm said, is to develop and operate up to 5 gigawatts of additional data center capacity.

item.title: Backrooms is a certified blockbuster with a $38 million opening day
item.description: The Kane Parsons' film Backrooms is expected to earn up to $90 million in its opening weekend after pulling down $38 million on Friday alone. That's not only above expectations, but absolutely...

item.title: ‘What a joke’: Github Copilot’s new token-based billing spurs consternation among devs
item.description: The golden age of Microsoft's Github Copilot appears to be at an end.

item.title: Welcome to Night Vale host Cecil Baldwin shares his tech pet peeves
item.description: Cecil Baldwin's résumé includes appearances on Gravity Falls, narrating the documentary Scream, Queen! My Nightmare on Elm Street, and performing as part of the New York Neo-Futurists theater...

item.title: Meta is reportedly developing an AI pendant
item.description: Meta seems to be making big bets on AI-powered hardware.

item.title: I put Google’s 24/7 AI assistant Gemini Spark to work, and it’s actually pretty useful
item.description: Gemini Spark helps automate everyday tasks, from inbox summaries to local event planning, but it’s unclear why Google made it a separate product.

item.title: Microsoft is threatening legal action for disclosing exploits
item.description: Microsoft is facing criticism for its handling of zero-day exploits. Someone going by the name Nightmare Eclipse has been publicly feuding with the company, posting proof-of-concept exploit code....

item.title: Two of our favorite art TVs are more than 40 percent off this weekend
item.description: Art TVs aren’t exactly known for being affordable, which is why Woot’s latest sale is notable. Now through June 26th, the retailer is discounting Samsung’s 2025 Frame TV and Hisense’s 2025...

item.title: As the browser wars heat up, here are the hottest alternatives to Chrome and Safari in 2026
item.description: We’ve compiled an overview of some of the top alternative browsers available today aiming to challenge Chrome and Safari.

item.title: Do You Actually Need to Pay for Transcription Software?
item.description: I tested Wispr Flow and various AI-powered transcription software to see whether you should bother subscribing or stick with free services.

item.title: PAN-OS GlobalProtect Authentication Bypass (CVE-2026-0257) Under Active Exploitation
item.description: Palo Alto Networks has warned that a recently disclosed medium-severity security flaw impacting PAN-OS and Prisma Access has come under active exploitation in the wild. The vulnerability, tracked...

item.title: Coders are refusing to work without AI — and that could come back to bite them
item.description: While AI is helping coders produce code faster, it may not be producing better code, researchers warn. And that could cause problems down the road for them.
